pubmed_links: [Shi et al. (2011) Common variants on 8p12 and 1q24.2 confer risk of schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22037555/); [Ma et al. (2013) Evaluating risk loci for schizophrenia distilled from GWAS in Han Chinese from Central China](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22584866/); [Sarnowski et al. (2022) Meta-analysis of GWAS identifies ancestry-specific associations underlying circulating total tau levels](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35396452/)
summary: NSD3 (WHSC1L1), a histone H3K36 methyltransferase at chromosome 8p12, was identified as a schizophrenia risk locus in genome-wide association studies in Han Chinese populations, with a common variant reaching genome-wide significance (rs16887244, P=1.27×10⁻¹⁰); a subsequent GWAS also identified NSD3 among genes with important brain function through its association with circulating total tau levels. (emerging evidence)
